diagrams.title: Operator Precedence Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows the hierarchy of operators from highest to lowest precedence in C
diagrams.purpose: Understanding which operators are evaluated first in expressions without explicit parentheses

diagrams.title: Types of Statements in C
diagrams.description: Classification of all statement types in C programming
elements: Jump Statements - break, continue, return, goto
diagrams.purpose: Understanding different categories of statements and their use in program flow control

diagrams.title: Expression Evaluation Steps
diagrams.description: Shows how a complex expression is evaluated step by step
elements: Step 1: Identify parentheses and evaluate sub-expressions
Step 2: Apply operator precedence to group operators
Step 3: Evaluate operators of same precedence using associativity
Step 4: Apply side effects in correct order (sequence points)
Example: x = a + b * c - d / e
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the systematic approach to evaluating complex expressions correctly

diagrams.title: Prefix vs Postfix Increment
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the difference between prefix (++x) and postfix (x++) increment operators
elements: Prefix ++x: x becomes 6, expression value is 6
Postfix x++: expression value is 5, then x becomes 6
Both have same final effect on x but different return values
diagrams.purpose: Clarifying the timing difference between prefix and postfix operators for correct usage
